steps.prompt: Which word is the first main verb?

hints.senex: 'senex' is a noun/adjective meaning 'old man / old'
hints.putet: 'putet' is a verb in the subordinate clause

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — not 'esse'
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand) — not 'esse'
hints.exeo, exire, exii, exitum: These are the parts of 'exire' (to go out) — not 'esse'

hints.to prune: 'to prune' is 'putare' in a different sense — here it means 'to think'
hints.to fight: 'to fight' is 'pugnare' — do not confuse with 'putare'
hints.to be able: 'to be able' is 'posse' — 'putet' comes from 'putare' (to think)

hints.Present indicative: Present indicative would be 'putat' — 'putet' with -e- is subjunctive
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'putaret' — 'putet' is present subjunctive
hints.Future indicative: Future would be 'putabit' — 'putet' is present subjunctive in a relative clause of characteristic

hints.pugno, pugnare, pugnavi, pugnatum: These are the parts of 'pugnare' (to fight) — not 'putare'
hints.possum, posse, potui, —: These are the parts of 'posse' (to be able) — not 'putare'
hints.punio, punire, punivi, punitum: These are the parts of 'punire' (to punish) — not 'putare'

hints.to possess: 'to possess' is 'possidere' — 'posse' means to be able
hints.to demand: 'to demand' is 'poscere' — similar sounding but different from 'posse'

steps.prompt: What tense and mood is 'posse' in this sentence?

hints.Present indicative: 'posse' is not a finite form — it is an infinitive in indirect statement after 'putet'
hints.Perfect infinitive: Perfect infinitive would be 'potuisse' — 'posse' is present
hints.Imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'posset' — 'posse' is the infinitive

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to place) — not 'posse'
hints.possideo, possidere, possedi, possessum: These are the parts of 'possidere' (to possess) — not 'posse'
hints.posco, poscere, poposci, —: These are the parts of 'poscere' (to demand) — not 'posse'

hints.to see: 'to see' is 'videre' — similar sounding but different from 'vivere'
hints.to conquer: 'to conquer' is 'vincere' — do not confuse with 'vivere'

hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see) — not 'vivere'
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer) — not 'vivere'
hints.vito, vitare, vitavi, vitatum: These are the parts of 'vitare' (to avoid) — not 'vivere'

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'annus' — 'annum' is accusative (duration of time)
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'anno' — 'annum' is accusative expressing duration

hints.1st: 1st declension accusative ends in -am — 'annus, anni' is 2nd declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension accusative ends in -em — 'annum' with -um is 2nd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension accusative ends in -um too, but 'annus, anni' is 2nd declension

translation: No one is so old that he does not think he can live one more year.
